text: Databases are essential for storing and organizing data. In relational databases, data is arranged in tables consisting of rows and columns. You can retrieve information using SQL (Structured Query Language) by writing queries to select, insert, or update data. Data modeling involves planning how tables and relationships are structured to avoid duplication. Indexes are special structures that speed up queries by allowing the database to find data faster, like an index in a book. In contrast, NoSQL databases are more flexible, storing data in formats like documents or graphs rather than tables. They are often used for large-scale applications where relationships are less rigid.
